# Feature: Run Reporting — `exportFile` Adapter

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-001
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_run_reporting.md`

Add a generic `exportFile(fileName, content, mimeType)` method to `IStorageAdapter` so any in-memory string can be saved to disk by the user. This is the single I/O boundary used by the run reporting feature (and any future text-export features such as JSON exports). It must work consistently on both VS Code (save dialog → `fs.writeFile`) and Web (`Blob` → `a[download]` download), and follow the established `Result<T, E>` adapter contract.

This feature ships only the adapter plumbing — no UI, no report content. It unblocks FEAT-003 / FEAT-004 / FEAT-005 (which generate the HTML strings and call this method).

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature introduces no persisted entities. It defines a single new method shape and reuses existing adapter infrastructure.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`IStorageAdapter.exportFile`** (new method on existing interface in [packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts](packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts))
  - `fileName` (`string`) — suggested file name presented in the save dialog (VS Code) or used as the `download` attribute (Web). The caller is responsible for sanitization and extension; the adapter does not modify it.
  - `content` (`string`) — UTF-8 text content to write. Binary export is out of scope; callers needing binary use the existing `IFileAdapter.writeBinaryFile` path.
  - `mimeType` (`string`) — content type used for the Web Blob and (where relevant) the save-dialog filter on VS Code (e.g., `text/html`, `application/json`).
  - **Returns**: `Promise<Result<{ filePath: string; fileName: string }, string>>`. `filePath` is the absolute path on VS Code; on Web it is `''` (no path concept). `fileName` echoes the actual name used (which may differ from the requested name on VS Code if the user chose another in the dialog).

### Relationships

- N/A.

### Indexes & Constraints

- N/A.

### Behavioral Constraints

- VS Code: if the user cancels the save dialog, the adapter returns `err('Export cancelled by user')`. File-system errors return `err(<reason>)`.
- Web: cannot detect dialog cancellation reliably. Always treats the click as success and resolves `ok({ filePath: '', fileName })`. Returns `err(<reason>)` only if `Blob` construction or URL handling throws.
- The implementation must not log the `content` argument (per the standing rule against logging sensitive data — content may contain headers / auth values from the run).

---

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` — Add `exportFile` to `IStorageAdapter`.
- `packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.ts` — Add a mock implementation that records `(fileName, content, mimeType)` calls and returns a configurable `Result`.
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts` — Post `exportFile` message to extension via `sendAndWait` and surface the response.
- `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts` — Add `exportFile` case: open `vscode.window.showSaveDialog` (using suggested name + an extension filter derived from `mimeType`), `await fs.promises.writeFile`, return `Result`.
- `packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts` — Implement Blob-based download (`createObjectURL` → hidden `a[download]` → `click()` → `revokeObjectURL`).
- `packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.test.ts` (or extend existing) — Verify the mock records calls and respects the configured result.
- `packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts` — Tests for the new `exportFile` handler.
- `packages/web/src/test/adapters/webAdapter.test.ts` — Tests for the Blob/download path.

---

## 4. Dependencies

- None. This is the foundational feature in the run reporting plan; FEAT-002 onwards depend on it.

---

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Extend `IStorageAdapter` with `exportFile` and update the mock

- [ ] **Implementation**: In [packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts](packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts) add the new method to the `IStorageAdapter` interface with a JSDoc block describing the signature, dialog/cancel semantics, and the platform-specific notes (VS Code returns absolute path; Web returns `''`). In [packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.ts](packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.ts) implement `exportFile` as a `vi.fn()`-style spy that resolves to `ok({ filePath: '/mock/path/' + fileName, fileName })` by default, with a configuration option to override the result for error-path tests.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Mock-adapter tests asserting (1) default success path returns `Result.isOk`, (2) configurable error path returns `Result.isErr` with the supplied message, (3) the spy records the exact `fileName / content / mimeType` triple.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new interface method (signature, return semantics, cancel-on-vscode behavior, no-cancel-detection-on-web caveat). Update the comment block at the top of `IStorageAdapter` if it lists capabilities.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core typecheck` succeeds (the new method shape compiles); `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- mockAdapter` passes.

### TASK-002: Implement `exportFile` on the VS Code side (webview adapter + extension handler)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In [packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts](packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts) add an `exportFile` method that posts `{ type: 'exportFile', data: { fileName, content, mimeType } }` via the existing `sendAndWait` correlation pattern (same shape used by `exportCollection`). In [packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts](packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts) add an `exportFile` case that (a) calls `vscode.window.showSaveDialog` with `defaultUri` derived from the suggested file name and a `filters` entry derived from `mimeType` (map `text/html` → `{ HTML: ['html'] }`, `application/json` → `{ JSON: ['json'] }`, default → `{ All: ['*'] }`), (b) on cancel returns `err('Export cancelled by user')`, (c) `await fs.promises.writeFile(uri.fsPath, content, 'utf-8')`, (d) returns `ok({ filePath: uri.fsPath, fileName: path.basename(uri.fsPath) })`. Errors are logged with operation context (file path, length of content) but not the content itself.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: In [packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts](packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts) cover (1) happy path: dialog returns a URI → `writeFile` called with content + utf-8 → response is `ok({ filePath, fileName })`, (2) cancel path: dialog returns `undefined` → `writeFile` not called → response is `err('Export cancelled by user')`, (3) write-error path: `writeFile` throws → response is `err(<wrapped message>)` and the error is logged, (4) MIME-type-to-filter mapping for `text/html`, `application/json`, and an unknown type (falls back to `All: ['*']`).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc for the new handler case + adapter method. Update any relevant developer-facing doc/README under `packages/vscode/` that lists supported message types.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/vscode test -- MessageHandler` passes. Manual smoke (post-implementation): in the VS Code extension dev host, call the adapter via the browser console to write a small `.html` file and confirm the save dialog appears with the suggested name.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

### TASK-003: Implement `exportFile` on the Web side (Blob download)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In [packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts](packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts) add `exportFile`: construct `new Blob([content], { type: mimeType })`, create an object URL, append a hidden `<a>` with `download = fileName` and `href = url` to `document.body`, call `.click()`, remove the element, and `URL.revokeObjectURL(url)`. Resolve `ok({ filePath: '', fileName })`. Wrap the entire body in `try/catch` and return `err((e as Error).message)` on failure.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: In [packages/web/src/test/adapters/webAdapter.test.ts](packages/web/src/test/adapters/webAdapter.test.ts) using jsdom, mock `URL.createObjectURL` and `URL.revokeObjectURL` as `vi.fn()` and spy on `document.createElement('a')` (or capture the appended anchor) to assert (1) `Blob` is constructed with correct content + type, (2) the anchor has `download === fileName` and `href === <created object URL>`, (3) `.click()` is called, (4) the anchor is removed and `revokeObjectURL` is called, (5) the result is `ok({ filePath: '', fileName })`, (6) when `createObjectURL` throws, the result is `err(<message>)`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the web adapter method noting the no-cancel-detection caveat. Update any developer-facing README under `packages/web/` that lists adapter capabilities.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/web test -- webAdapter` passes. Manual smoke: open the web app dev server, call the adapter via the browser console with `text/html` content, confirm the file downloads with the requested name.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — the new method does not interact with credentials.
- **Error Handling**: Strict `Result<T, string>` discipline. Cancellation on VS Code is an `err`, not a thrown exception. Web cannot distinguish cancel from success and never throws on a successful click. Adapter callers (FEAT-003 onward) treat `err` as a notification-worthy failure (no retry).
- **Logging**: VS Code handler logs (a) operation start with `fileName` + content length, (b) write-error with file path and `error.message` — never log `content`. Web adapter logs the error message only on catch.
